message:
fix(store): add modification_count to Entry for WATCH correctness and fix missing dirty increments

Add a monotonic `modification_count` field to `Entry` that is bumped on
every write operation, enabling reliable WATCH-based optimistic locking
(detects ABA scenarios that timestamp-only tracking misses).

Changes:
- Add `modification_count: u64` field to `Entry` struct, initialized to 0
- Add `bump_mod_count()` and `touch_write()` helper methods on Entry
- Replace all `entry.touch(now_ms)` calls with `entry.touch_write(now_ms)`
  in write paths (APPEND, SETRANGE, SETBIT, HSET, HDEL, HINCRBY, HSETNX,
  HINCRBYFLOAT, LPUSH, RPUSH, LSET, LINSERT, SADD, ZADD, ZREM,
  ZINCRBY, ZREMRANGEBYSCORE, ZREMRANGEBYRANK, ZREMRANGEBYLEX)
- Carry forward and increment modification_count in
  `internal_entries_insert` when replacing an existing key
- Add `key_modification_count()` public accessor for WATCH consumers
- Add missing `self.dirty` increments for: LCS dest writes, XCLAIM with
  FORCE, SORT STORE, SCRIPT LOAD, SCRIPT FLUSH, FUNCTION LOAD,
  FUNCTION DELETE, FUNCTION FLUSH, CONFIG SET, and RESTORE

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
